title: Restore a 1910 magician-troupe postcard: clarify the dark front photo and make the handwritten back legible, delivered as two book/social-ready files
one_line_ask: Digitize and restore one 1910 magician-troupe postcard — clarify and lighten the dark sepia front photo and sharpen the contrast of the handwritten back so the description is legible — and deliver each side as its own clean, deskewed, print-and-social-ready file.

issues: Slight tilt and scanner margin visible (acceptable per brief, for deskew/crop).
Faintness and low contrast of handwriting (intended per brief).
Some stains and foxing on paper (realistic for age, not a flaw).
Stamp box is very faint.
Handwriting is slightly uneven and hard to read in places (expected per brief).
panel.text_found:
POST CARD
SINGAPORE
MAR
1910
Front row, my mother and Aunt Lillie with the Company at the Royal — Singapore, March 1910

issues: Slight warm/sepia tone cast across entire card — acceptable per brief as pipeline target
Minor tilt (~2-3 degrees clockwise) — within spec, designer to deskew
Faint brown stain/foxing mark on left side of card — period-appropriate aging, not an artifact
Stamp box upper right is mostly empty (stamp appears missing/very faint) — consistent with used postcard
Postmark day numeral partially illegible ('MAR [?] 1910') — intentional faintness per brief
Paper texture shows authentic yellowing and edge wear — correct
No severe AI artifacts detected
No real-world brands or trademarks visible
panel.text_found: POST CARD | SINGAPORE / MAR [?] / 1910 | Front row, my mother and Aunt Lillie with the Company at the Royal — Singapore, March 1910

issues: Slight clockwise tilt (~2-3°) present — acceptable per brief
Shadow crush in left and right flanking figures reduces costume detail — acceptable per brief
Blown highlight from stage footlight/spotlight at top-center — acceptable per brief
Faint cool/greenish scanner cast visible over warm sepia — acceptable per brief
Scanner margin visible around image edges — acceptable per brief
Minor corner wear/foxing on print — adds authenticity
Table/prop on stage-left slightly cuts into composition but does not obscure subject
Central figure (magician in tailcoat) is sharp, well-centered, and clearly the focal point
All ~8-9 supporting performers in period Asian-tour costuming visible and correctly positioned
No CGI/render artifacts detected — reads as genuine photographic emulsion grain
No real-world brands, logos, or trademarks visible
